title: Sustained damage cycle
principles: Sustained damage describes useful offensive output maintained across repeated active and recovery cycles.
Cycle continuity and active damage output must both be supported because a stable operating cycle does not by itself establish meaningful sustained damage.
Active output, recurring recovery costs, mechanical application continuity, and validated contact must remain separate properties.
Reload friction affects output across repeated short cycles, while ammunition pressure may limit continuation across longer use.
Only damage components explicitly participating in the evaluated repeated cycle may contribute to sustained-damage evidence.
Cycle-adjusted output must remain theoretical when contact or application validity is not sufficiently defined.
Qualitative sustained-damage classifications require a supplied comparison context and must not use universal thresholds.

strong: Active damage output remains favorable after accounting for the supported recurring recovery cycle.
No supplied interruption or application limitation substantially undermines repeated offensive pressure.
moderate: The attack pattern maintains useful cycle-adjusted output, but one or more supplied limitations noticeably reduce repeated pressure.
The limiting factors must remain explicitly identified.
limited: Active output, recurring interruptions, or application restrictions provide limited evidence for maintaining useful damage across repeated cycles.
Operational continuity alone is insufficient to raise this classification.
undetermined: Active damage output, cycle timing, application continuity, or comparison context is insufficiently defined.
The sustained-damage relationship cannot be classified confidently.

interruption_limited: Record reload, recharge, preparation, or another recurring recovery cost as a limiting factor.
Use the normalized interruption profile without reconstructing its recovery model inside this concept.
application_limited: Record restrictions in attack-event or damage-instance frequency separately from interruption friction.
Do not infer practical application frequency from trigger type or fire rate alone.
contact_limited: Reduce practical confidence only when validated contact evidence applies to the same mode and damage components.
Do not infer continuous contact from beam, automatic, held, or sustained-input behavior.
reserve_limited: Record ammunition pressure as a long-duration limitation without merging it into reload friction.
Do not infer mission-long sustainability from ammo pickup amount alone.
persistent_component_present: Include damage continuing during a recovery interval only when its duration, activation, and temporal overlap with that interval are explicitly supplied.
Do not assume every radial, chained, delayed, or secondary component persists.
multiple_cycle_states: Preserve separate active-output and interruption states when cadence, damage, consumption, or application changes during use.
Do not reduce spool-up, variable charge, staged behavior, or conditional output to one constant cycle.
multiple_modes_present: Evaluate sustained damage independently for every relevant attack mode.
Do not combine their active output, interruptions, or reserve costs into one synthetic cycle.

exceptions: Do not infer sustained damage from automatic, held, continuous, beam, or high-fire-rate behavior alone.
Do not infer favorable sustained damage from a long active window or low reload friction without supported active damage output.
Do not merge reload friction, application limitations, contact limitations, and ammunition pressure into one undifferentiated factor.
Do not assume uninterrupted target contact throughout the active window.
Do not select sustained_damage as the primary job from cycle continuity or cycle-adjusted output alone.
